name: org.machanism.machai:genai-client
description:
GenAI Client (org.machanism.machai:genai-client) is a provider-agnostic Java library (MachAI module) that standardizes integration with Generative AI / LLM backends. It defines the core SPI org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai for conversation-oriented prompting and execution: initialize from org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator (init), accumulate user prompts (prompt) and system instructions (instructions), attach local (java.io.File) or remote (java.net.URL) resources as inputs (addFile), register host-executed tools/functions for model function-calling (addTool), execute the run (perform) and retrieve token usage metrics (usage via org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Usage). Providers may keep session state and can be reset via clear(); inputs can be logged to disk via inputsLog(File); host tool execution can be scoped using setWorkingDir(File).

Provider resolution and lifecycle: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getProvider(String chatModel, Configurator conf) resolves providers from an identifier formatted as "Provider:Model" (e.g., "OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini"). If the provider prefix is omitted, it defaults to the None provider. Provider class resolution supports short names mapped to org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.<providerLower>. <Provider>Provider or fully-qualified class names. The manager sets conf key chatModel to the model portion and calls provider.init(conf). GenAIProviderManager also aggregates per-run Usage records via addUsage(Usage) and can log an aggregate summary with logUsage(); usage aggregation is stored in a static list and is not synchronized.

Built-in providers:
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.openai.OpenAIProvider: functional provider using the OpenAI Java SDK Responses API. Supports prompts, system instructions, file inputs (uploading local files via Files API or referencing URLs), embedding generation (embedding(text, dimensions)), tool/function calling (register tools with addTool and dispatch function calls to host ToolFunction handlers), iterative tool-call resolution (follow-up requests until final text), and usage reporting (input tokens, cached input tokens, output tokens). Configuration keys: chatModel (required), OPENAI_API_KEY (required), OPENAI_BASE_URL (optional OpenAI-compatible endpoint), GENAI_TIMEOUT (seconds; optional), MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS (optional), MAX_TOOL_CALLS (optional).
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.codemie.CodeMieProvider: EPAM CodeMie integration that performs an OIDC token request to obtain an OAuth2 access token (password grant if GENAI_USERNAME contains "@", otherwise client_credentials). It sets OPENAI_BASE_URL to CodeMie API base URL and OPENAI_API_KEY to the access token, then delegates based on chatModel prefix: gpt-* (or blank) -> OpenAIProvider; gemini-* -> GeminiProvider; claude-* -> ClaudeProvider. Optional AUTH_URL overrides the token endpoint.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.none.NoneProvider: offline/no-op provider used as default when provider prefix is omitted. It accumulates prompt text and can write prompts to a configured inputsLog file and write instructions to instructions.txt alongside the log. perform() always returns null; embedding is unsupported (throws UnsupportedOperationException); tools and files are ignored; usage is always zero.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.gemini.GeminiProvider and org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.claude.ClaudeProvider: scaffolds/placeholders; most operations are not implemented yet.

Host-integrated tools (function calling): the library provides an SPI org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools to install tool sets into a provider. Tools are discovered via java.util.ServiceLoader and applied using org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader. Tool sets can optionally receive a Configurator for runtime placeholder substitution in strings using ${...}.
Included tool sets:
- org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FileFunctionTools: registers read_file_from_file_system, write_file_to_file_system, list_files_in_directory, get_recursive_file_list. Paths are interpreted relative to the working directory passed at invocation time.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.WebFunctionTools: registers get_web_content (HTTP GET with optional headers, timeout, charset, selector extraction, and optional textOnly rendering) and call_rest_api (generic HTTP method with headers/body). Supports basic auth via URL userInfo and ${...} placeholder substitution in URLs/headers.
- org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.CommandFunctionTools: registers run_command_line_tool and terminate_process. Command execution uses ProcessBuilder with working-directory confinement under the project root, bounded output capture (LimitedStringBuilder), environment parsing, and a deny-list heuristic via CommandSecurityChecker (OS-specific denylist resources denylist/unix.txt or denylist/windows.txt, extendable via configuration property ft.command.denylist). terminate_process throws a ProcessTerminationException with an exitCode.

Adapter/decorator support: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiAdapter is a delegating GenAIProvider base class that forwards all operations to a configured delegate provider, enabling cross-cutting concerns (logging, metrics, retries) while preserving the GenAIProvider contract.

Typical usage: resolve a provider via GenAIProviderManager.getProvider("OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini", conf); optionally install tools via FunctionToolsLoader.getInstance().setConfiguration(conf) and applyTools(provider); setWorkingDir(new File(".")) if tools are used; set instructions and prompts; attach files if needed; call perform(); then read provider.usage() and/or aggregate with GenAIProviderManager.logUsage().

constructors.description: Resolves and instantiates a GenAIProvider based on a provider/model identifier. The identifier is typically formatted as "Provider:Model" (for example, "OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini"). If the provider part is omitted, the default provider is used (NoneProvider). Providers are created via reflection (public no-arg constructor), the Configurator is updated with chatModel (model-only part), and provider.init(conf) is called.

examples.description: Creates and uses an OpenAI-backed provider instance, runs a prompt, and reads back token usage.

constructors.description: Returns a singleton loader that discovers FunctionTools implementations via java.util.ServiceLoader and can apply them to a GenAIProvider. The loader can also distribute a Configurator to tools for runtime placeholder substitution (for example, ${...} in web headers or command strings).

examples.description: Installs all host-integrated tools (file, web, command) into a provider instance.

constructors.signature: java.lang.String org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.codemie.CodeMieProvider.getToken(java.lang.String url, java.lang.String username, java.lang.String password) throws java.io.IOException
constructors.description: Requests an OAuth 2.0 access token from the configured CodeMie OpenID Connect token endpoint. Uses password grant when username contains "@" and client_credentials otherwise. Intended for configuring OPENAI_API_KEY for downstream OpenAI-compatible calls.

examples.description: Obtains an access token directly from the token endpoint (typically used internally by CodeMieProvider).

features.name: GenAIProvider.perform
features.description: Executes the provider using accumulated prompts, instructions, attached files, and registered tools to produce a response. Concrete providers may perform multiple iterations when tool calls are involved (for example, OpenAIProvider executes tool calls, appends tool outputs, and re-queries until a final response is produced).

examples.description: Runs a model invocation using prompts and system instructions.

features.name: GenAIProvider.addTool
features.signature: void org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai.addTool(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String description, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai.ToolFunction function, java.lang.String... paramsDesc)
features.description: Registers a host-executed tool/function that a provider may expose to the model for function calling. ToolFunction receives an Object[] and returns an Object result; the exact argument structure is provider-specific. In OpenAIProvider, the tool handler receives (0) a com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode of parsed arguments and (1) a java.io.File working directory.

examples.description: Registers a simple tool that reads a JSON argument and returns a string result.

features.name: GenAIProvider.embedding
features.description: Computes an embedding vector for the input text using a provider-specific embedding model and dimensionality. Implemented by OpenAIProvider; unsupported by NoneProvider (throws UnsupportedOperationException).

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai provider = /* OpenAIProvider */;
java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vec = provider.embedding("hello world", 1536L);
examples.description: Computes an embedding vector for downstream semantic search or similarity matching.

features.name: GenAIProviderManager.logUsage
features.description: Aggregates token usage records across runs and logs a summary of input, cached input, and output tokens. Usage aggregation is stored in a static list and is not synchronized.

examples.description: Logs total token usage across multiple provider invocations.

features.name: FunctionToolsLoader.applyTools
features.description: Discovers FunctionTools implementations via ServiceLoader and registers all tool functions into a provider. Tool sets typically include file tools, web tools, and command execution/termination tools.

examples.description: Discovers and applies all tool installers to a provider.

features.name: WebFunctionTools.getWebContent
features.description: Host-side tool implementation for get_web_content: performs an HTTP GET to a URL, applies optional headers, timeout, charset decoding, optional CSS selector extraction, and optional HTML-to-text rendering. Supports basic authentication via URL userInfo converted to an Authorization header and supports ${...} placeholder substitution in URLs/headers when a Configurator is supplied.

examples.description: Registers web tools so the provider can expose them to function-calling capable models.

customizations.name: GenAIProvider implementations
customizations.description: Add a new provider by implementing org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai with a public no-arg constructor. It can then be resolved by GenAIProviderManager either via a short name (org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.<lowercase>.<Name>Provider) or a fully-qualified class name in the provider portion of the model identifier.

examples.description: Skeleton of a new provider implementation.

customizations.name: FunctionTools SPI
customizations.description: Extend the host-executed tool surface by implementing org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools and registering it via ServiceLoader (META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools). Tool installers register one or more tools into a GenAIProvider via GenAIProvider.addTool. Installers may also accept a Configurator to resolve ${...} placeholders at runtime.

examples.description: Adds a new host-side function tool installer.

customizations.name: Command deny-list extension
customizations.description: Extend the built-in OS-specific command deny-list used by CommandFunctionTools/CommandSecurityChecker via configuration property ft.command.denylist. The property is applied as a String.format template where the first argument is the built-in denylist content, enabling prepend/append of custom rules.

examples.description: Appends extra keyword rules to the built-in deny-list.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai
studs.description: Provider contract for integrating a concrete GenAI backend. Implementations collect prompts/instructions, attachments, and tools, execute perform() to produce a response, and report per-run token usage.

examples.description: Uses the GenAIProvider contract without binding to a specific vendor implementation.

methods: void addTool(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String description, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Genai.ToolFunction function, java.lang.String... paramsDesc)

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: SPI for installing host-side function tools into a GenAIProvider, typically discovered via ServiceLoader and applied through FunctionToolsLoader. Includes helper support for resolving ${...} placeholders using a Configurator.

examples.description: Discovers and applies all tool installers to a provider.

examples.title: Resolve provider and run a prompt
Enable host tools for function calling
Use CodeMieProvider for enterprise authentication
